question: Are both lion-head door knockers replaced with dragon-head knockers?
Are the dragon-head knockers correctly aligned with the perspective and surface of the wooden doors?
Are the two knight statues replaced with traditional Chinese guardian lion statues?
Do the guardian lion statues match the gray stone texture and weathering of the surrounding castle walls?
Are the guardian lion statues properly scaled and grounded on their bases without floating or sinking?
Are all three triangular pennants replaced with Chinese-style red vertical streaming banners?
Do the new banners align with the original wind direction (blowing towards the right)?
Are the wooden door planks preserved intact, excluding the replaced knockers?
Are the stone archway, castle walls, and ivy vegetation preserved without structural changes?
Are the sky, clouds, and foreground dirt path unchanged?
Is the lighting on the replaced objects (knockers, statues, banners) consistent with the overcast environment?
Is the edited image free of visual artifacts, such as warped geometry or unnatural blurring, around the replaced objects?
